config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena hak individu adalah batas yang mencegah kekuasaan disalahgunakan. Kalau hak pribadi terlalu mudah dikorbankan atas nama “kepentingan umum”, yang sering terjadi justru kelompok lemah menjadi korban. Bagi saya, yang ideal adalah keseimbangan: masyarakat dilindungi, tetapi hak dasar setiap orang tetap dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, individual rights are the whole point of limiting government and protecting people from mob rule, majority pressure, and abuses done in the name of the "greater good." That said, rights are not absolute in every situation, because public safety and shared rules matter, but the burden should always be on society to justify any restriction very carefully. When people say "society first" too loosely, it often becomes a shortcut for trampling unpopular minorities or weak groups.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, kita sering melihat alasan “demi umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan penggusuran, pembatasan kritik, atau perlakuan tidak adil terhadap kelompok kecil, jadi saya justru curiga kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, rasa aman, dan perlakuan yang adil; kalau tidak, yang kuat akan selalu menang atas yang lemah. Menurut saya, ukuran yang benar bukan “masyarakat dulu, individu belakangan,” melainkan apakah pembatasan itu benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak merampas martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of language has historically been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. The burden should be on society to prove that a limit on rights is necessary, narrow, and temporary, not just convenient or popular. At the same time, I do not think individual rights mean zero obligations to other people; public health, safety, and basic laws do matter. But once "the common good" becomes a blank check, the people with the least power usually pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penggusuran, pembungkaman kritik, atau diskriminasi terhadap kelompok kecil, jadi saya tidak mau memberi cek kosong pada negara atau mayoritas. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar dan diuji apakah pembatasannya benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak merendahkan martabat orang. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat justru mudah berubah menjadi alat bagi yang kuat untuk menekan yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should generally outrank the individual, because from a U.S. perspective that framing has too often been used to justify abuses in the name of order or the majority. The real issue is not whether society matters, but whether a restriction on rights is necessary, narrow, and genuinely tied to preventing harm rather than just making life easier for the powerful. I do not buy the argument that "the common good" should be the default trump card, because that is exactly how unpopular people and minority groups get steamrolled. Society has responsibilities, but individual rights should remain the starting point, not the thing that gets sacrificed first.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
